title: W300-B01 Web shell adapter boundary
description:
Type: Feature

Goal
  Add the smallest rendering boundary that turns a GuiShellPacket into web-shell markup or DOM instructions without selecting a broad architecture prematurely.

Design
  - Add a focused crate/module only if layering requires it.
  - Accept GuiShellPacket as input.
  - Render project tree, editor, diagnostics, lifecycle, run output, COM capability, command summary, focus/accessibility metadata, and capability footer.
  - Keep no-claim flags visible: DOM audit false unless tested, filesystem false, native runtime false, COM runtime false.

Tests
  - Boundary consumes GuiShellPacket rather than rebuilding state.
  - Render includes all major W290 shell surfaces.
  - No parked TUI modules are imported.

Evidence
  - cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace
  - Render/snapshot command for the adapter boundary.

Closure
  - [ ] Adapter boundary consumes GuiShellPacket.
  - [ ] Major shell surfaces render.
  - [ ] No untested host capability is claimed.
close_reason: Added oxide-webshell thin adapter boundary over oxide-core::GuiShellPacket and gui-web-shell-adapter-boundary lab scenario. Adapter renders major shell surfaces, command/focus/accessibility summaries, no-claim flags, and parked TUI isolation without selecting a framework or claiming DOM smoke/audit/filesystem/native/COM support. Evidence: cd crates && cargo fmt --all && cargo test --workspace passed; cargo run -p oxide-guilab -- render gui-web-shell-adapter-boundary. Frozen OxVba cfg(kani) warnings only.

title: W320-B01 Native filesystem service seam
description:
Type: Implementation

Goal
  Add a native filesystem service seam that writes and reads a test-owned thin-slice project copy without touching checked-in fixtures.

Design
  - Add a native/filesystem provider type in the GUI crate stack.
  - Copy fixture files into a test-owned temporary project directory.
  - Save edited module text through the provider.
  - Reload module text from disk and prove it matches the saved edit.
  - Keep browser-limited persistence as a separate unsupported provider.

Tests
  - Native provider writes Module1.bas in a temp project copy.
  - Reload returns the saved source.
  - Checked-in fixture content remains unchanged.
  - Browser-limited provider still reports no direct filesystem persistence.

Evidence
  - cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p oxide-core.
  - Targeted test names proving disk write/read/reload.

Closure
  - [ ] Disk write/read is covered by tests.
  - [ ] Checked-in fixture mutation is guarded by tests.
  - [ ] Browser-limited filesystem remains disabled.
close_reason: Added NativeFilesystemDocumentPersistence in oxide-core with provider label native-filesystem, disk-backed load/save, all-supported lifecycle capabilities, and structured IO persistence errors. Added tests that copy examples/thin-slice/Module1.bas into test-owned temp project directories, write/reload temp Module1.bas, verify lifecycle save acknowledges disk write, and verify checked-in fixture content remains unchanged. BrowserLimitedPersistence still reports filesystem disabled for load/save/reload. Evidence: cd crates && cargo fmt --all && cargo test -p oxide-core (46 tests passed).

close_reason: Added GuiKeyboardMap/GuiKeyboardContext/keybinding collision projection in oxide-core with explicit contexts, no default context collisions, explicit cross-context allowance for Enter, and disabled reasons preserved from command palette. Added gui-keyboard-contexts-baseline scenario rendering contexts, keybindings, collision counts, disabled reasons, and host-independent policy note. title: W330-B02 Immediate service contract packet
description:
Type: Implementation

Goal
  Add an OxIde-side Immediate service packet that can represent unavailable/native-service-missing states and future request/response rows without fake responses.

Design
  - Include optional Immediate session identity.
  - Include request text and response/event rows for future service data.
  - Include command availability and disabled reasons.
  - Include no-fake-responses and no-native-runtime/COM flags for current profiles.

Tests
  - Browser-disabled packet has no responses.
  - Native-service-missing packet has no responses and a service-missing reason.
  - JSON round-trip preserves no-fake-response flags.

Evidence
  - cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p oxide-core.

Closure
  - [ ] Immediate packet is serializable.
  - [ ] Unavailable profiles do not invent responses.
  - [ ] Runtime/COM claims remain false.
close_reason: Added ImmediateServiceResponseKind, ImmediateServiceResponse, and ImmediateServicePacket in oxide-core. Packet carries optional Immediate/runtime session identity, provider kind, optional request text, command status, response rows, fake_responses flag, and runtime/COM no-claim flags. Browser-disabled and native-service-missing constructors produce no responses and no fake/runtime/COM claims. Evidence: cd crates && cargo fmt --all && cargo test -p oxide-core (55 tests passed), including JSON round-trip and native-service-missing no-fake-response tests.

title: W330-B01 Runtime service contract packet
description:
Type: Implementation

Goal
  Add an OxIde-side runtime service packet that can represent browser-disabled, simulated, native-service-missing, and future native-service-available run states without claiming untested execution.

Design
  - Include runtime session identity as optional until a real native service exists.
  - Include workspace/project/run target correlation.
  - Include provider kind and command availability.
  - Include lifecycle/activity/output/error event rows.
  - Include no-claim flags for real execution, native runtime, and COM runtime.

Tests
  - Packet round-trips through JSON.
  - Browser-disabled and native-service-missing packets keep real execution false.
  - Simulated packet remains explicitly simulated.
  - No COM runtime claim appears in any current packet.

Evidence
  - cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p oxide-core.

Closure
  - [ ] Runtime packet is serializable.
  - [ ] Missing native service is distinct from browser unsupported.
  - [ ] Simulated output remains labeled simulated.
close_reason: Added RuntimeServiceProviderKind, RuntimeServiceCommandStatus, and RuntimeServicePacket in oxide-core. Packet covers browser-disabled, native-service-missing, and simulated current states with optional runtime_session_id, workspace/run target correlation, command availability, event rows, and no-claim flags for real execution, native runtime, and COM runtime. Tests prove JSON round-trip, native-service-missing distinct from browser unsupported, simulated remains labeled simulated, and all current packets keep COM/runtime/real-execution claims false. Evidence: cd crates && cargo fmt --all && cargo test -p oxide-core (53 tests passed).
